This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"registerPool(address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"_asset":"The asset of the pool","_maxRelayFeeBPS":"The maximum relay fee in basis points","_minimumDepositAmount":"The minimum deposit amount for the asset","_pool":"The address of the Privacy Pool contract","_vettingFeeBPS":"The deposit fee in basis points"}},"relay((address,bytes),(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[8]),uint256)":{"params":{"_proof":"The `WithdrawProof` struct containing the withdarawal proof signals","_scope":"The Pool scope to withdraw from","_withdrawal":"The `Withdrawal` struct"}},"removePool(address)":{"params":{"_asset":"The asset of the pool"}},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. rls":["bzz-raw://937fe0104ee8062d493daeca05010dff6d4d6001a7782a75a4f550dbaa24771d","dweb:/ipfs/QmXAv5ffUhjiC44ys6fNExsiPTBpFcJiex33swtZweWcbN"]}},"version":1}

params._value: The amount of asset to deposit

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

params._maxRelayFeeBPS: The maximum relay fee in basis points
params._minimumDepositAmount: The minimum deposit amount for the asset
params._pool: The address of the Privacy Pool contract
params._vettingFeeBPS: The deposit fee in basis points

params._proof: The `WithdrawProof` struct containing the withdarawal proof signals
params._scope: The Pool scope to withdraw from

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

returns._root: The ASP root at the index

params._asset: The asset of the pool to update
params._maxRelayFeeBPS: The new max relay fee in basis points
params._vettingFeeBPS: The new vetting fee in basis points

params._ipfsCID: The IPFS v1 CID of the association set data

returns._index: The index of the newly added root

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

assetConfig(address).notice: Returns the configuration for an asset

associationSets(uint256).notice: Returns the association set data at an index

constructor.notice: Disables initializers. Using UUPS upgradeability pattern

deposit(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Make an ERC20 deposit into the Privacy Pool

deposit(uint256).notice: Make a native asset deposit into the Privacy Pool

registerPool(address,address,uint256,uin.notice: Register a Privacy Pool in the registry

removePool(address).notice: Remove a Privacy Pool from the registry

rootByIndex(uint256).notice: Returns an ASP root by index

scopeToPool(uint256).notice: Returns the configured pool for a scope

updatePoolConfiguration(address,uint256,.notice: Updates the configuration of a specific pool

updateRoot(uint256,string).notice: Push a new root to the association root set

usedPrecommitments(uint256).notice: Returns a boolean indicating if the precommitment has been used

windDownPool(address).notice: Irreversebly halt deposits from a Privacy Pool
